title: VALI: optimize for 500+ variant COMPONENT_SETs
description: Implement 5 optimization strategies: structural fingerprinting, scan filtering, OPS chunking, template OPS, decision table
notes: 5 optimizations: (1) Structural fingerprinting in scan.figma.js — fingerprints all variants, scans 1 per unique structure. (2) SKIP_OK filtering — already-correct nodes emitted as compact summaries. (3) Chunked execution in process.figma.js — CHUNK_SIZE batches prevent timeout. (4) Template OPS — single template op expands to all 500 variants at runtime with {VARIANT_ID}/{CHILD:N:ID} placeholders. (5) Decision table in vali.prompt.md — ≤20 standard, 21-100 fingerprint, >100 fingerprint+sample cap.
close_reason: All 5 VALI optimization strategies implemented

title: MIMR: optimize for 500+ variant COMPONENT_SETs
description: Implement 5 optimization strategies: variant sampling, REST depth limiting, plugin-side audit script, batch node resolution, chunked bulk-update
notes: 5 optimizations implemented: (1) Variant sampling — detect >20 variant COMPONENT_SETs, sample primary axis only. (2) REST depth param — use depth=3 for large sets. (3) New audit.figma.js plugin script — walks tree server-side, returns only bound nodes. (4) Batched resolve.figma.js — Promise.all in chunks of 50. (5) Chunked bulk-update — pre-collect work queue, process in batches of 100. Decision table added to MIMR prompt: REST for ≤20, REST+sampling for 20-100, plugin audit for >100.
close_reason: All 5 optimization strategies implemented and documented in MIMR prompt

title: MIMR: FDS-Button font+color+gloss NV binding
description: Bind NV for all 308 FDS-Button-Update variants: typography (textStyleId per Size), content fill (fds-on-btn-* per Colour on Button Label + icon VECTORs), background fill (fds-btn-* on Primary-type variants), gloss effect (fds-specular-const-gloss-on-surface on fill-bearing variants). User confirmed: Large+Regular→lead-bold, gloss-on-surface for all 3 fill colours, bind icon fills, Primary-only for bg fill.
notes: DONE: 308/308 variants written in 4 batches (Large/Regular/Small/Tiny, ~50s each). Operations per variant: textStyleId (Size-tiered: Large+Regular→lead-bold/16px, Small→regular-bold/14px, Tiny→small-bold/12px), content fill NV (setBoundVariableForPaint on Button Label TEXT + both icon VECTORs, per Colour axis 6 tokens), background fill NV (Primary-type only, per Colour: accent/default/alt-accent), gloss effectStyleId (gloss-on-surface, same for all 3 fill-bearing colours per user choice). QA: 3 sampled variants confirmed NV=TS across all properties. Note: getLocalEffectStyles() sync API gives false empty result in QA script — use async version next time.

description: User invoked /odin-9000 with frame J30dy2G5oy3VtjhasE8VUt#5:974. notes:

MODI (5:974): 9 deletes, 7 instance swaps (FDS-site-header 29:158, FDS-ProgressBar Valid 29:192, 4x FDS-Input Default 29:201/228/245/262, FDS-Button-Update Primary Large Continue 29:199), 0 failures.

title: mimr: re-apply card/page surface tokens after VALI restructure
description:
After VALI restructured Test-for-ODIN frame 0:3 to auto-layout, three styling regressions need MIMR to apply tokens:

1. Root frame 0:3 lost its fill (applyALSettings wiped it). Needs a page background — likely fds-surface or fds-bg.
2. New {col / pattern} wrapper 12:244 (bonus body: title + ProgressBar) needs the surface fill + corner radius that was on the deleted Rectangle 7. Was bound to var/fds/fds-on-primary + fds-border-radius-input-default in MODI run.
3. Sectional padding may want to be applied to {col / section} 12:279 and root for proper edge insets.

Also opportunity: audit all 5 new AL frames to verify gap tokens applied by VALI are correct and check whether any other token gaps exist (paddings, fills) on the restructured frames.

Frame: https://www.figma.com/design/J30dy2G5oy3VtjhasE8VUt/Test-for-ODIN?node-id=0-3
notes:
## MIMR run — Test-for-ODIN 0:3 — complete

**Approach:** ad-hoc plan (MIMR Phase 2 Option b) rather than mapping-rules.md authoring, since this was a one-shot stylisation pass on 5 specific nodes after VALI restructure.

**Inspection:** all 5 VALI-renamed AL frames had gap tokens ✓ from prior pass, zero fills/padding/radius. Text nodes 1:14 and 1:11 had raw colours (no tokens).

**Phase 3 — 11 writes (0 failures):**
| Node | Property | TS path |
|---|---|---|
| 0:3 root | fill | var.fds.fds-background |
| 12:244 bonus card | fill | var.fds.fds-on-primary |
| 12:244 bonus card | borderRadius (4) | fds-round-const.containers.standard.fds-round-const-container-reg |
| 12:244 bonus card | paddingTop/Bottom/Left/Right | spacing.fds-spacing-const.container.fds-spacing-const-container-card |
| 1:14 "Choose your bonus" | fill | var.fds.fds-on-surface-hi |
| 1:11 "or" | fill | var.fds.fds-on-background |
| 12:279 body section | paddingLeft/Right | spacing.fds-spacing-const.container.fds-spacing-const-container-canvas-reg |

All 11 writes used both TS sharedPluginData + auto-NV resolution by canonical name (`getLocalVariablesAsync` + `setBoundVariable` / `setBoundVariableForPaint`).

**Post-write fix:** Root frame 0:3 had `sizingV: FIXED` (517px), preserved by VALI's `restoreSizing`. After MIMR added paddings the content needed ~682px → overflow into canvas (Login/or were floating outside the frame). Changed `layoutSizingVertical = 'HUG'` to absorb overflow.

**Gotcha — bulk-update.figma.js can't match by node-id:**
The script matches rules by layer-name pattern (exact/contains/regex). Our two `{col / pattern}` frames (12:244 + 12:245) share the same name, so per-id targeting requires either temporary renames or inline writes. Used inline writes here. Future enhancement opportunity: add `nodeIds: [...]` matcher to the rule schema.

**Visual outcome:**
- Page reads cleanly top-to-bottom: header → bonus card → 4 inputs → Continue → or → Login
- `fds-background` resolves to white (light theme); `fds-on-primary` (also white) means bonus card blends with page bg
- For dark-theme presentation, the user would switch variable mode or use `fds-surface-variant` for the card

**Files NOT touched:** mapping-rules.md (ad-hoc rules per Option b — not persisted to disk).
close_reason: MIMR complete — 11 TS+NV writes on 5 nodes (page bg + card surface/radius/padding + 2 text fills + section canvas gutter) + 1 root sizing fix. All bindings auto-resolved to local FDS variables. Page renders cleanly within frame bounds.

title: vali: convert Test-for-ODIN frame 0:3 to semantic auto-layout
description: After MODI parse run (fds-designer-ehe), frame J30dy2G5oy3VtjhasE8VUt#0:3 has 8 INSTANCE children + 2 TEXT children + 1 background RECTANGLE laid out via absolute positioning. VALI must convert the root frame (and any internal wrappers) to VERTICAL auto-layout with semantic {direction/role} naming so MIMR can apply spacing tokens. INSTANCEs are read-only and must be skipped per VALI rules.
notes:
## VALI run — Test-for-ODIN 0:3 — complete

**Pre-step (inline):** deleted Rectangle 7 (1:13), reordered 0:3 children to visual order [site-header, body, "or", Login], reordered 1:31 children to [inputs, Continue].

**Phase 2/3 structural (process.figma.js, 18 ops, 0 failures):**
- 8 ungroups: 1:28, 1:25, 1:29, 1:24, 1:23, 1:22, 1:21, 1:27
- 5 renames: 1:26→{col / pattern}, 1:30→{col / pattern}, 1:31→{col / group}, 1:32→{col / section}, 0:3→{col / group}
- 5 al-conversions: 1:26→12:244, 1:30→12:245, 1:31→12:278, 1:32→12:279, 0:3→0:3 (in-place)

**Phase 2b tokens + annotations (10 ops total):**
- 5 itemSpacing tokens bound: 12:244 (vPattern), 12:245 (vPattern), 12:278 (vGroup), 12:279 (vSection), 0:3 (vGroup)
- 5 Dev Mode annotations added

**Gotcha — script gap-var IDs are file-specific:**
`scripts/process.figma.js` has gap variable IDs hardcoded to file `Ahvbwk0dUHeHazrQX2XtGd` (VariableID:8094:5962x). For other files those IDs don't resolve. Test-for-ODIN file has its own local copy at VariableID:2:409–412. Had to retry tokens in a separate use_figma call with the correct local IDs. This is a script portability issue worth fixing — process.figma.js should look up gap vars by name not hardcoded id.

**Final structure:**
```
0:3 {col / group}
├── 6:7362 FDS-site-header
├── 12:279 {col / section}
│   ├── 12:244 {col / pattern}   (Choose your bonus + ProgressBar)
│   └── 12:278 {col / group}     (inputs + Continue button)
│       ├── 12:245 {col / pattern} (4× FDS-Input)
│       └── 6:14625 Continue button
├── 1:11 "or"
└── 6:14627 Login button
```

**Hand-off to MIMR:**
- root 0:3 lost its fill (applyALSettings sets fills=[]); needs a page background applied
- 12:244 bonus body lost the surface (was Rectangle 7); needs surface fill + corner radius re-applied
- typography on "Choose your bonus" (1:14) and "or" (1:11) may need contrast fixes once background settles
close_reason: VALI complete — 23 ops (8 ungroups + 5 renames + 5 al + 5 tokens + 5 annotations) + 3 pre-step ops (1 delete + 2 reorder). 0 failures. Auto-layout flows cleanly top-to-bottom. New AL frame IDs captured for MIMR handoff. Script gap-var hardcoding issue documented.

title: odin-9000: Parse Test-for-ODIN wireframe — analyse shapes & resolve to FDS library
description:
Frame: https://www.figma.com/design/J30dy2G5oy3VtjhasE8VUt/Test-for-ODIN?node-id=0-3

Wireframe analysis run via MODI Parse mode:
- Scan frame, classify placeholders
- Suggest FDS library matches per shape
- If no library match → stylise inline with TS + NV tokens
- Ask user on low-confidence resolution
- Ask which library to use if none detected
notes:
## MODI Parse run — Test-for-ODIN frame 0:3 — complete

**Library used:** 🧣 DS Fabric Components (WIP) (user linked it after detection prompt)

**Phase 1 — scan:** 0:3 frame inspected via get_metadata. 8 wireframe shapes identified (1 top bar, 1 progress bar, 4 inputs, 1 primary CTA, 1 text link).

**Phase 2 — resolve:**
- Library matches found via search_design_system + REST component_sets endpoint for variant axes
- 3 low-confidence asks raised: top bar (no exact match → user supplied 'FDS-site-header'), progress bar (variants are password-flavoured → user accepted trade-off), Login text (text vs Teritary button → user picked Teritary)
- Component map updated with FDS-site-header + FDS-ProgressBar entries

**Phase 3 — swap (swap.figma.js, 8 ops, 0 failures):**
- 1:2 Rectangle 1 → FDS-site-header (Status=Logged-out, Break-point=<1024px) → 6:7362
- 1:15 Rectangle 8 → FDS-ProgressBar (Property 1=Valid) → 6:7935
- 1:5/6/7/8 → FDS-Input ×4 (State=Default, Theme=Surface) → 6:13702/13737/13754/13771
- 1:9 Rectangle 6 → FDS-Button-Update (Large/Primary/default/On surface) → 6:14625
- 1:12 Login → FDS-Button-Update (Regular/Teritary/surface default/On surface) → 6:14627

**Phase 4 — cleanup (single use_figma call):**
- Deleted 8 redundant children: 1:3 Logo, 1:4 x, 1:16 Rectangle 9, 1:17/18/19/20 input labels, 1:10 Continue text
- Set instance label overrides on all 6 inputs/buttons via inner 'Label' / 'Button Label' text nodes
- Bound var/fds/fds-on-primary to Rectangle 7 fill + fds-border-radius-input-default to all corners
- Applied Inter Semi Bold 16 to 'Choose your bonus' (1:14), Inter Regular 12 to 'or' (1:11) — no local FDS type styles found

**Files changed:** .github/prompts/modi/data/component-map.md (added FDS-site-header + FDS-ProgressBar entries)

**Known follow-ups (not done in this run):**
- FDS-ProgressBar shows password-strength visual instead of true 27% progress — needs a different progress component or inline 2-rect stylisation if accuracy matters
- Bonus card outline (Rectangle 7) now blends into white background (no stroke); add subtle border if visual separation needed
- 'Choose your bonus' + 'or' used raw font properties because no published FDS text styles were exposed via getLocalTextStylesAsync — when FDS typography styles are published, MODI cache can grow a TS_STYLES section to bind them properly
close_reason: MODI Parse run complete — 8 wireframe shapes resolved to FDS library components (FDS-site-header, FDS-ProgressBar, 4× FDS-Input, FDS-Button-Update Primary + Teritary), 8 redundant children removed, 6 label overrides applied, Rectangle 7 + texts stylised with FDS variables/typography. 0 swap failures.

All bindings are TS-only (0 native variables). 37 unique SUD tokens mapped: 19 direct FDS equivalents (51%), 13 near/semantic (35%), 5 no FDS equiv (14%). Key FDS targets: fds-spacing-const-badge-sm-{h,v} for padding, fds-spacing-const-ui-gap for itemSpacing, fds-sb-state-*/product-*/stats-* for colors, fds-paragraphs-{micro,tiny,small,regular}-bold for typography. Open decisions: pill radius (no FDS const), 2px spacing (below FDS floor), opacity alpha deltas.

title: schema: document implicit output contract for MIMR / VALI / SAGA
notes:
Inferred from skill instructions (no prior run logs available). Output contracts as understood:

MIMR outputs:
- Phase 2: tree-format audit report (stdout) — rows: { id, name, type, parentName, tokenBindings, rawBoundVars/resolved, hasTokens, hasBoundVars }
- Phase 2: summary line — { total, withTS, withNV, conflicts, unbound }
- Phase 3: audit log table — { result, count } with per-entry: nodeId, rule, key, value, status (applied/nv-auto/component-value/skipped/error)
- No file output — all output is conversational/in-chat

VALI outputs:
- Phase 1 (Analyse): OPS plan — array of { nodeId, name, currentType, action, proposedName, layoutMode, direction }
- Phase 2+3 (Execute): Figma write via process.figma.js; no file output
- Confirmation gate before any write

SAGA outputs:
- {name}.html — semantic HTML mirroring layer hierarchy
- {name}.css — vanilla CSS with --fds-* custom properties
- {name}.module.css — CSS Modules variant
- CSS var naming: last segment of NV variable name prefixed --

All inferred. UNVERIFIED — needs validation against real run logs.
Action: run each skill once on a real frame and compare actual output to this shape. Update these notes with diffs.

title: arch: separate skill contracts from implementation (defer)
notes:
Rationale: skill files currently mix contract (what the skill does, inputs/outputs, success criteria) with implementation (phase names, exact tool calls, file paths, grep strategies). This makes the contract implicit and brittle when Figma MCP or bd CLI changes.

Proposed split per skill:
  contract.md  — inputs, outputs, success criteria, handoff schema (stable)
  {skill}.prompt.md — implementation details (volatile)

ODIN-9000 depends only on the contract, not the implementation. This separation would let odin branch on real outcomes without reading full skill files each time.

DEFER until: 5+ real pipeline runs have been completed and failure modes are concrete.
Prior to acting: review issue fds-designer-0ro (output contract schema) — the contract.md content comes directly from that work.
